exports.name: enableNotifications
exports.signature: () -> Promise<{granted: bool, reason?: 'denied' | 'unsupported' | 'requires_pwa_install'}>; gated to be called only from a user click handler; on success, subscribes via PushManager and persists via section-2's savePushSubscription

exports.name: sendTurnNotification
exports.signature: (gameId: string, recipientUid: string, payload: {title: string, body: string, url: string}) -> Promise<{sent: bool, reason?: 'no_subscription' | 'permission_denied' | 'send_failed'}>; reads the recipient's push subscription via section-2 and dispatches a VAPID-signed Web Push request to the recipient's endpoint

exports.name: notificationStatus
exports.signature: () -> {permission: 'default' | 'granted' | 'denied', subscribed: bool, platform_supports_push: bool, ios_requires_home_screen_install: bool}

invariants: Section-4 calls sendTurnNotification immediately after a successful submitHandful when the round transitions to 'waiting_for_opponent' (i.e., when the current player has just submitted and the opponent now owes a turn).
If sendTurnNotification returns sent: false, the game still functions — the opponent will see their turn the next time they open the app. The notification is an enhancement, not a precondition.
Section-4 reads notificationStatus to decide whether to surface the 'Enable notifications' affordance or the 'Add to Home Screen first' prompt on iOS.
